body.thing: the inference proxy is a leaf: the routing core imports nothing from the domain layer, so device -> domains -> agentic_loop -> device is no longer a cycle
body.intention: I intend that no module of the inference routing core (device, rules_engine, sources, models_registry, connections, policy, dimensions, routing_buckets) imports the domain layer or the agentic loop at ANY nesting depth including function-local imports, that BaseDomain carries no routing method, and that the proxy builds its own RouteRequest from the routing layer's own task_class->tier bridge

body.why: A proxy routes and dispatches; a domain runs an agentic loop that dispatches TO it. BaseDomain.select() inverted that — a routing method on a proxy-consumer — so the proxy had to import a domain to choose a model, and the domain's loop had to lazily re-import the proxy to run. The lazy function-local import hid the cycle from every import-time check, so the guard must read the import graph with ast.walk rather than trust module-level imports.
